---
title: "Fire Metrics Report (By Question)"
author: ""
date: "`r format(Sys.Date(), '%Y-%m-%d')`"
output:
  pdf_document:
    latex_engine: xelatex
    number_sections: true
    toc: true
geometry: margin=0.8in
header-includes:
  - "\\usepackage{pdflscape}"
  - "\\usepackage{booktabs}"
  - "\\usepackage{caption}"
  - "\\captionsetup{font=small}"
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = FALSE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE,
  fig.align = "center"
)

library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(lubridate)
library(zoo)
library(ggplot2)
library(trend)
library(forecast)

# ---- User edit: where the fire plot PNGs live ----
# If your PNGs are in the same folder as the Rmd, keep as ".".
fire_plot_dir <- "."

plot_path <- function(fname) {
  p <- file.path(fire_plot_dir, fname)
  if (!file.exists(p)) {
    # fallback: try common relative folder used in the script
    p2 <- file.path("plots", "fire", fname)
    if (file.exists(p2)) return(p2)
  }
  p
}

landscape_begin <- function() cat("\\n\\\
\\begin{landscape}\\n")
landscape_end   <- function() cat("\\n\\\
\\end{landscape}\\n")
```

```{r data-and-metrics, include=FALSE}
# ============================================================
# Load data + compute metrics
# ============================================================
# NOTE: This block contains all code needed to reproduce the metrics,
# but it is hidden from the PDF.

library(readr)

# 1) Directory
# Update if needed
# town_dir <- "/mnt/ceph/erichs/git/WWL-climate-extraction/data/ID_data/ID/"
# stopifnot(dir.exists(town_dir))

# ---- If you prefer sourcing a script, do it here instead of inlining ----
# source("fire_metrics_script.R")

# If you are knitting on a machine that has the data, uncomment the block below.
# Otherwise, keep your current approach (e.g., sourcing a metrics script).

# files <- list.files(town_dir, pattern = "\\.csv$", full.names = TRUE)
# if (length(files) == 0) stop("No CSV files found in: ", town_dir)
#
# file_index <- tibble(
#   file = files,
#   filename = basename(files),
#   stem = str_remove(filename, "\\.csv$")
# ) %>%
#   mutate(
#     buffer = if_else(str_detect(stem, "10mibuffer"), "10mibuffer", "none"),
#     stem_nobuf = str_remove(stem, "10mibuffer"),
#     source = str_extract(stem_nobuf, "(?<=_)(era5land|gridmet|nclim)$"),
#     town   = str_remove(stem_nobuf, "_(era5land|gridmet|nclim)$")
#   )
#
# town_tbl <- file_index %>%
#   mutate(data = purrr::map(file, \(f) read_csv(f, show_col_types = FALSE))) %>%
#   select(town, buffer, source, data) %>%
#   unnest(data)
#
# town_tbl <- town_tbl %>%
#   mutate(
#     date_str = str_extract(`system.index`, "^\\d{8}"),
#     date     = as.Date(date_str, format = "%Y%m%d"),
#     year     = year(date),
#     month    = month(date),
#     wateryear = if_else(month >= 10, year + 1L, year),
#     doy      = yday(date),
#     season4  = case_when(
#       month %in% 3:5  ~ "MAM",
#       month %in% 6:8  ~ "JJA",
#       month %in% 9:11 ~ "SON",
#       TRUE            ~ "DJF"
#     ),
#     yday = yday(date),
#     mday = mday(date),
#     wyday = ifelse(
#       year(date) == wateryear & leap_year(year(date)),
#       yday + 92,
#       ifelse(
#         year(date) == wateryear,
#         yday + 91,
#         ifelse(leap_year(wateryear - 1), yday - 274, yday - 273)
#       )
#     )
#   )
#
# # ---- Christine-style daily-climo thresholds and annualfire table ----
# wateryearselect <- 1980:2025
# df <- town_tbl %>%
#   filter(source == "gridmet", wateryear %in% wateryearselect) %>%
#   select(town, buffer, source, `system.index`, date, year, month, wateryear, yday, wyday, mday, season4,
#          bi, erc, fm100, fm1000, pr, tmmn, tmmx, vpd, vs)
#
# df5day <- df
# df5day[, c("bi","erc")] <- rollmean(df[, c("bi","erc")], 5, align = "center", na.pad = TRUE)
#
# quibble <- function(x, q = c(0.9, 0.95), dropNA = TRUE) {
#   tibble(x = quantile(x, q, na.rm = dropNA), q = q)
# }
#
# daily9625_firequants <- df5day %>%
#   filter(wateryear %in% 1996:2025) %>%
#   group_by(month, mday) %>%
#   reframe(across(c(bi, erc), ~ quibble(.x, q = c(0.9, 0.95), dropNA = TRUE))) %>%
#   pivot_longer(cols = c(bi, erc), names_to = "var", values_to = "qb") %>%
#   mutate(
#     quantile = qb$q,
#     value = as.numeric(qb$x),
#     name = paste0("dailyclimo9625_", var, "_q", quantile)
#   ) %>%
#   select(month, mday, name, value)
#
# df_fireclimo <- df5day %>%
#   left_join(daily9625_firequants, by = c("month","mday")) %>%
#   pivot_wider(names_from = name, values_from = value) %>%
#   mutate(
#     high_bi90  = bi  > dailyclimo9625_bi_q0.9,
#     high_erc90 = erc > dailyclimo9625_erc_q0.9,
#     high_bi95  = bi  > dailyclimo9625_bi_q0.95,
#     high_erc95 = erc > dailyclimo9625_erc_q0.95,
#     sev_bi90  = pmax(bi  - dailyclimo9625_bi_q0.9,  0, na.rm = TRUE),
#     sev_erc90 = pmax(erc - dailyclimo9625_erc_q0.9, 0, na.rm = TRUE)
#   )
#
# max_run <- function(x) {
#   x <- as.integer(x); x[is.na(x)] <- 0L
#   r <- rle(x)
#   if (!any(r$values == 1L)) 0L else max(r$lengths[r$values == 1L])
# }
#
# first_sustained_wyday <- function(high_vec, wyday_vec, k = 3) {
#   hv <- as.integer(high_vec); hv[is.na(hv)] <- 0L
#   if (length(hv) < k) return(NA_integer_)
#   roll <- zoo::rollapply(hv, width = k, FUN = sum, align = "left", fill = NA)
#   idx <- which(roll == k)
#   if (length(idx) == 0) NA_integer_ else wyday_vec[min(idx)]
# }
#
# annualfire <- df_fireclimo %>%
#   group_by(town, buffer, wateryear) %>%
#   summarise(
#     firehighdays_bi90  = sum(high_bi90,  na.rm = TRUE),
#     firehighdays_erc90 = sum(high_erc90, na.rm = TRUE),
#     firehighdays_bi95  = sum(high_bi95,  na.rm = TRUE),
#     firehighdays_erc95 = sum(high_erc95, na.rm = TRUE),
#     fireseverity_bi90  = sum(sev_bi90,  na.rm = TRUE),
#     fireseverity_erc90 = sum(sev_erc90, na.rm = TRUE),
#     firesummerhighdays_bi90  = sum(high_bi90  & season4 == "JJA", na.rm = TRUE),
#     firesummerhighdays_erc90 = sum(high_erc90 & season4 == "JJA", na.rm = TRUE),
#     firestart_wyday_bi  = first_sustained_wyday(high_bi90,  wyday, k = 3),
#     firestart_wyday_erc = first_sustained_wyday(high_erc90, wyday, k = 3),
#     firemaxrun_bi  = max_run(high_bi90),
#     firemaxrun_erc = max_run(high_erc90),
#     fireseasonlength_bi = {
#       idx <- which(high_bi90 %in% TRUE)
#       if (length(idx) < 2) NA_integer_ else (max(wyday[idx]) - min(wyday[idx]))
#     },
#     fireseasonlength_erc = {
#       idx <- which(high_erc90 %in% TRUE)
#       if (length(idx) < 2) NA_integer_ else (max(wyday[idx]) - min(wyday[idx]))
#     },
#     firepeak_bi  = max(bi,  na.rm = TRUE),
#     firepeak_erc = max(erc, na.rm = TRUE),
#     firep95_erc  = as.numeric(quantile(erc, 0.95, na.rm = TRUE)),
#     .groups = "drop"
#   )

# ---- IMPORTANT ----
# This Rmd assumes an object named `annualfire` exists after the above (or sourced) code.
# If you already create annualfire by sourcing a script, keep that approach.
```

```{r check-required-objects, include=FALSE}
if (!exists("annualfire")) {
  stop("Object `annualfire` not found. Either source your metrics script in the hidden chunk, or paste your annualfire-building code there.")
}

annualfire_long <- annualfire %>%
  pivot_longer(cols = -c(town, buffer, wateryear), names_to = "metric", values_to = "value")
```

# Fire metrics framed by survey-style questions

## Q1: Has the overall frequency of high fire-weather days changed?

**Metric:** annual count of days exceeding the daily (1996-2025) 90th percentile threshold (ERC and BI).

```{r q1-landscape-begin, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_begin()
```

```{r q1-plot, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q1: Overall frequency (annual high days)'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q1_timeseries_firehighdays_erc90.png"))
```

```{r q1-dist, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q1: Distribution of annual ERC q90 high days'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q1_distribution_firehighdays_erc90.png"))
```

```{r q1-landscape-end, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_end()
```

## Q2: Has the frequency of high fire-weather days changed in summer?

**Metric:** annual count of high days occurring in JJA.

```{r q2-landscape-begin, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_begin()
```

```{r q2-plot, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q2: Summer frequency (JJA high days)'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q2_timeseries_firesummerhighdays_erc90.png"))
```

```{r q2-landscape-end, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_end()
```

## Q3: Has fire-weather potential become more severe?

**Metric (proxy):** annual peak ERC and 95th percentile ERC (tail intensity).

```{r q3-landscape-begin, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_begin()
```

```{r q3-plot1, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q3 proxy: Annual peak ERC'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q3proxy_timeseries_firepeak_erc.png"))
```

```{r q3-plot2, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q3 proxy: Annual ERC 95th percentile'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q3proxy_timeseries_firep95_erc.png"))
```

```{r q3-landscape-end, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_end()
```

## Q4: Is the fire season starting earlier?

**Metric:** first sustained run (k=3) of high fire-weather days, reported as water-year day (Oct 1 = 1).

```{r q4-landscape-begin, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_begin()
```

```{r q4-plot1, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q4: Fire season start timing (ERC q90, wyday)'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q4_timeseries_firestart_wyday_erc.png"))
```

```{r q4-plot2, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q4: ERC start day (wyday) early vs late'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q4_periodcompare_firestart_wyday_erc.png"))
```

```{r q4-landscape-end, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_end()
```

## Q5: Are high fire-weather spells lasting longer?

**Metric:** maximum consecutive run length of high days; optional season length (last - first high day).

```{r q5-landscape-begin, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_begin()
```

```{r q5-plot1, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q5: Max consecutive high-fire run length (ERC q90)'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q5_timeseries_firemaxrun_erc.png"))
```

```{r q5-plot2, echo=FALSE, fig.cap='Q5 optional: Season length (ERC q90: last - first high day)'}
knitr::include_graphics(plot_path("Q5_timeseries_fireseasonlength_erc.png"))
```

```{r q5-landscape-end, results='asis', echo=FALSE}
landscape_end()
```

# Trend summaries (Sen slope + Mann-Kendall)

```{r trend-table, echo=FALSE}
fire_trends <- function(startyears = c(1980, 1985, 1990, 1995, 2000, 2005, 2010, 2015, 2020)) {
  metrics_to_trend <- c(
    "firehighdays_bi90","firehighdays_erc90",
    "firesummerhighdays_bi90","firesummerhighdays_erc90",
    "firestart_wyday_bi","firestart_wyday_erc",
    "firemaxrun_bi","firemaxrun_erc"
  )

  dfL <- annualfire_long %>%
    filter(metric %in% metrics_to_trend, !is.na(value))

  out <- list()

  for (sy in startyears) {
    tmp <- dfL %>%
      filter(wateryear >= sy) %>%
      group_by(town, buffer, metric) %>%
      summarise(
        sen_slope = sens.slope(value)$estimates,
        p_value   = mk.test(value)$p.value,
        n_years   = n(),
        .groups = "drop"
      ) %>%
      mutate(TimePeriod = paste0(sy, "-2025"))

    out[[as.character(sy)]] <- tmp
  }

  bind_rows(out)
}

fire_slopes <- fire_trends()

fire_slopes %>%
  arrange(metric, p_value) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 4)
```

# ARIMA Monte Carlo: Is the observed trend unusual under a no-trend time-series model?

This section uses an ARIMA (no drift) fitted to each full-record metric time series to generate Monte Carlo slope distributions. It then places the observed Sen slope into a 7-bin quantile category (Q1 = most negative, Q7 = most positive).

```{r arima-mc, include=FALSE}
# --- CONFIG ---
set.seed(2026)
n_sim <- 2000

metrics_for_arima <- c(
  Q1 = "firehighdays_erc90",
  Q2 = "firesummerhighdays_erc90",
  Q3 = "firep95_erc",
  Q4 = "firestart_wyday_erc",
  Q5 = "firemaxrun_erc"
)

safe_sen_slope <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  if (length(x) < 5) return(NA_real_)
  tryCatch(as.numeric(sens.slope(x)$estimates), error = function(e) NA_real_)
}

fit_arima_nt <- function(x) {
  x <- as.numeric(x)
  x <- x[is.finite(x)]
  auto.arima(x, allowdrift = FALSE, allowmean = TRUE)
}

simulate_slopes <- function(fit, n, len) {
  out <- numeric(n)
  for (i in seq_len(n)) {
    sim <- as.numeric(simulate(fit, nsim = len))
    out[i] <- safe_sen_slope(sim)
  }
  out
}

# Build per-location observed slopes + ARIMA slope distributions
arima_results <- list()

for (q in names(metrics_for_arima)) {
  met <- metrics_for_arima[[q]]

  obs_df <- annualfire %>%
    select(town, buffer, wateryear, value = all_of(met)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
    group_by(town, buffer) %>%
    summarise(
      n_years = n(),
      obs_sen_slope = safe_sen_slope(value),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(question = q, metric = met)

  # Monte Carlo per location
  mc_df <- annualfire %>%
    select(town, buffer, wateryear, value = all_of(met)) %>%
    filter(!is.na(value)) %>%
    group_by(town, buffer) %>%
    group_modify(~{
      x <- .x$value
      if (length(x) < 10) return(tibble(sim_id = integer(), sim_slope = numeric()))
      fit <- tryCatch(fit_arima_nt(x), error = function(e) NULL)
      if (is.null(fit)) return(tibble(sim_id = integer(), sim_slope = numeric()))
      sims <- simulate_slopes(fit, n_sim, length(x))
      tibble(sim_id = seq_along(sims), sim_slope = sims)
    }) %>%
    ungroup() %>%
    mutate(question = q, metric = met)

  # 7-quantile bins (global across all locations for that question)
  breaks <- quantile(mc_df$sim_slope, probs = seq(0, 1, length.out = 8), na.rm = TRUE)
  # guard against duplicates
  if (any(diff(breaks) <= 0)) {
    breaks <- seq(min(mc_df$sim_slope, na.rm = TRUE), max(mc_df$sim_slope, na.rm = TRUE), length.out = 8)
  }

  obs_df <- obs_df %>%
    mutate(
      qcat = as.character(cut(obs_sen_slope, breaks = breaks, labels = paste0("Q", 1:7), include.lowest = TRUE, right = FALSE))
    )

  arima_results[[q]] <- list(obs = obs_df, mc = mc_df, breaks = breaks)
}

obs_all <- bind_rows(lapply(arima_results, `[[`, "obs"))
mc_all  <- bind_rows(lapply(arima_results, `[[`, "mc"))
```

## ARIMA summary heatmap (Q1-Q7 categories)

```{r arima-heatmap, echo=FALSE}
# Show ARIMA quantile category by question and location
obs_plot <- obs_all %>%
  mutate(location = paste(town, buffer, sep = " - "))

ggplot(obs_plot, aes(x = question, y = location, fill = qcat)) +
  geom_tile(color = "white", linewidth = 0.25) +
  labs(
    title = "ARIMA delta diagnostics: Q1-Q7 category by question and location",
    x = "Question",
    y = "Location (town - buffer)",
    fill = "Qcat (1-7)"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10) +
  theme(
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 7),
    panel.grid = element_blank()
  )
```

## ARIMA slope-distribution panels (example: ERC-based metrics)

Each panel shows the Monte Carlo distribution of Sen slopes from ARIMA (no drift). The vertical line is the observed Sen slope.

```{r arima-dists, echo=FALSE}
# Keep it readable: pick a small set of locations to illustrate.
# Change the filter if you want all locations faceted.
example_locs <- obs_all %>%
  arrange(question, town, buffer) %>%
  group_by(question) %>%
  slice_head(n = 4) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  select(question, town, buffer)

mc_ex <- mc_all %>%
  inner_join(example_locs, by = c("question","town","buffer"))
obs_ex <- obs_all %>%
  inner_join(example_locs, by = c("question","town","buffer"))

mc_ex <- mc_ex %>% mutate(location = paste(town, buffer, sep = " - "))
obs_ex <- obs_ex %>% mutate(location = paste(town, buffer, sep = " - "))

p <- ggplot(mc_ex, aes(x = sim_slope)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40) +
  facet_grid(location ~ question, scales = "free_x") +
  geom_vline(data = obs_ex, aes(xintercept = obs_sen_slope), linewidth = 0.7) +
  labs(
    title = "ARIMA Monte Carlo slope distributions (examples)",
    x = "Sen slope (metric units per year)",
    y = "Simulation count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 10)

p
```

```{r arima-table, echo=FALSE}
obs_all %>%
  arrange(question, town, buffer) %>%
  select(question, town, buffer, metric, n_years, obs_sen_slope, qcat) %>%
  knitr::kable(digits = 4)
```
